title: boechera selbyi var. inyoensis
intro: Boechera selbyi var. inyoensis, also known as Inyo Mountains rockcress, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae), native to the Inyo Mountains of California. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have medicinal benefits in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Boechera selbyi var. inyoensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential as a medicinal plant.
